info.name: Insect Parade
info.attribution:
Layout, design, coding, and library image by LawDawg96. Released to the Public Domain under CC0.

Grass background by PublicDomainPictures, available under the Pixabay License from https://pixabay.com/photos/grass-lawn-field-meadow-grassy-84622/.

Insect icons by Anditii Creative are part of the Insects Icon Set and are available under the Noun Project License from https://thenounproject.com/browse/collection-icon/insects-191593/.

All other icons have attribution as listed in the About overlay.
info.description: It was the dress rehearsal for the annual Insect Parade and all the insects were lined up to start. it quickly became chaos as bugs started changing rows and getting out of step. Then it was every species for itself trying to get home before dark.
info.ruleText: Dozens of rule variations are available at https://boardgamegeek.com/boardgame/95/igel-argern/files.
info.helpText:
Players sit in seats and press the Restart button to begin.

Detailed usability information is found in the top left by hovering over the information box.

#enlarged.left: calc(var(--roomLeft) + (max(${PROPERTY enlargeX}px, 0px) + min(1px, max(0px, (0px - ${PROPERTY enlargeX}px))) * (800 - ${PROPERTY enlargeWidth} / 2) + min(1px, max(0px, (-1px - ${PROPERTY enlargeX}px))) * ((1600 - ${PROPERTY enlargeWidth}) - (800 - ${PROPERTY enlargeWidth} / 2))) * var(--scale))

aid.detail:
Set Up

Each player takes turns putting 1 piece at a time in the starting column (the dice on the left). Each row must be filled with 1 piece before starting a 2nd piece on an occupied row. Exception: if the only option is to play a piece on another one of your own pieces, you may put your starting piece anywhere. Each row must be filled with 2 pieces before starting a 3rd piece, etc. Continue until each player has placed all 4 pieces.

Movement

Players take turns moving a piece according to these 3 steps, which must be followed in order:

1. Roll Dice: Click the dice once to roll it.

2. Move Sideways: This step is optional. A player may move one of their own pieces in any row which is "on top" of the stack (to the far right in the space), or by itself, 1 row up or down.

3. Move Forward: Move 1 piece in the row that matches the dice forward 1 space toward the end column (the dice on the right). It does not matter which player the piece belongs to. The piece moved must be "on top" of the stack, or by itself, but if there are multiple qualifying pieces, the player can choose which piece to move.

Dark Square Exception: Pieces that are on a dark square cannot move (sideways or forward) until there are no pieces left behind them in any row.

5 Row Variant: If using the variation with 5 rows, when a player rolls a 6, that is treated as wild. The player can move a piece from any column except that a piece that moved sideways cannot also be moved forward using a wild. Optionally, by agreement, players may wrap sideways movement around the board between rows 1 and 5.

Game End

The game ends when the first player gets 3 pieces into the far right column.
